rulings.Case Title: HUDSON MOORE JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the negligence and strict liability claims (issues 1 and 2) and granted as to the false representation and punitive damages claims (issues 3 and 4). Plaintiff's objection to the late filing of the moving papers is overruled. CCP § 437c provides an opposing party the right to a minimum notice period of 75 days. Plaintiff received such notice and was not prejudiced by Rich-Tex's late filing of the moving papers. Plaintiff's January 27, 2010 declaration created a triable whether plaintiff used Rich-Tex asbestos-containing products. Rich-Tex's D'Amico objections are overruled as to paragraph 3 of plaintiff's declaration. Rich-Tex satisfied its burden of production regarding issues 3 and 4 by presenting plaintiff's factually-devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether Rich-Tex made an affirmative misrepresentation of material fact to plaintiff and whether Rich-Tex acted with fraud, malice, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. =(220/HEK)
